name: coding
description: Plan then implement a code change in the current repository. Plan stage is read-only; implement stage has write tools. Both stages have Magic Context memory tools: search prior solutions first, record durable learnings.

stages.prompt: FIRST: search project memory for prior work on this repo — run ctx_search with terms from the task (e.g. the feature area, file names, error text). Prior tickets may have already mapped the conventions, pitfalls, or even solved something similar; use what you find. Then study the repository and produce a concise implementation plan for the runtime task: which files change and how, repo conventions to follow, risks, and how to verify. End with the exact list of files to be created or modified.

Implement the upstream plan for the runtime task. If you hit anything non-obvious (build quirks, hidden coupling, project-specific conventions), check ctx_search for prior fixes before debugging from scratch. Make the code changes now, following the plan and repo conventions. When done, verify with bash: git add -A && git diff --cached --stat, and include that diff stat in your analysis. FINALLY: if you learned something durable about this repo that future tickets must know (a rule, constraint, gotcha, or convention — not task-specific detail), record it with ctx_memory(action="write").
